description: Same patient as mild-nausea-001, three turns into the conversation — already received the standard nausea education (ginger, small frequent meals, hydration). The new message is asking about a related but distinct concern. Exercises the prior-context wrapper that was the subject of v0.3.16's bugfix and currently has zero behavioral coverage. Pass criterion: AI does NOT repeat the education already given.

input.message: It's been a few more days. The nausea is still mild but now I'm noticing my appetite is way down — I'm eating maybe a third of what I normally do. Should I be worried about that?
input.prior_context:
Patient: I've had some mild nausea on and off for the past couple of days but I can still eat my regular meals. Is this normal?

Nurse: Mild nausea is one of the most common early side effects and usually settles within a couple of weeks. A few things that help: small frequent meals instead of large ones, sipping ginger tea or eating ginger candies, and staying well hydrated between meals rather than during. Avoid heavy/fatty foods. If it worsens, becomes persistent vomiting, or you can't keep fluids down, message us back right away.

Patient: Thanks — I'll try the ginger and smaller meals. I'll keep an eye on it.
